content: Thank you — that broadly tracks with what I had in mind, though before we run anything I'd want to clarify a couple of points so the output stands up to second-line review. On the two extra files you've added: am I right in understanding that the "classification errors" file would capture interactions the tool couldn't confidently sort, and the "quarantine" one would catch entries that don't match the expected structure of our Q3 extract? If so, I'd actually want both of those preserved as part of the evidentiary record — we can't have interactions silently dropped from the population, since our testing scope has to reconcile back to the full in-period record count. Could you confirm those two files retain the original interaction identifiers so we can tie them back to the source extract, and that nothing is excluded from the count without being accounted for somewhere? Also, a small proviso — for the fraud-flagged set, the regional leads shouldn't be receiving those at all, so please make sure the fraud file is kept entirely separate and not bundled into the package that goes out to the regional distribution list.
